uk: Свв. немовлят, за Христа вбитих у Вифлеємі
Преподобного отця нашого Маркелла, ігумена монастиря невсипущих.
saints.en: Holy Innocents, the Children slain for Christ in Bethlehem. Ven. Marcellus, Hegumen of the Monastery of the Unsleeping Ones

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На “Господи, взиваю” 6 стихир: 3 свята, 3 свв. младенців (далі - мчч.), Сл.: мчч., І нині: свята. Стиховня свята. По “Отче наш”: тропар мчч., Сл., І нині: свята. Відпуст середн. з помин. свята і мчч.
Утреня: по “Бог Господь”: тропар свята (2), Сл.: тр. мчч., І нині: тр. свята. Катизми черг., сідал. свята. Пс. 50. Канон свята та мчч. Катавасія тільки по 3-ій, 6-ій, 8-ій та 9-ій піснях – ірмос канону мчч. По 3-ій – конд.-ікос і сідал. мчч, Сл., І нині: свята. По 6-ій – конд.-ікос свята. По 9-ій – світил. мчч. (2), Сл., І нині: світил. свята. Стиховня свята. По “Отче наш”: тропар мчч., Сл., І нині: свята. Відпуст середній з помин. свята і мчч.
Часи: на всіх – тропар свята; на 3 та 9 – Сл.: тр. мчч. Кондаки: 1 та 6 – свята; 3 та 9 – мчч.
Літургія: антифони: перші два – свята, третій – загальний з приспівом свята; “Прийдіте, поклонімся”. Тропар свята, Сл., І нині: конд. свята; Прокімен, алилуя і причасний свята. Ап. – 1 Пт. 60 зач.; 3, 10-22. Єв. – Мр. 50 зач.; 11, 11-23. Замість “Достойно” – ірмос 9-ї пісні канону свята з його приспівом. Відпуст з помин. свята та мчч.

troparion_en.text: Your Nativity, O Christ our God,  /  has shone to the world the Light of knowledge;  /  for by it, those who worshipped the stars / were taught by a star to adore You,  / the Sun of Righteousness,1  /  and to know You, the Dayspring2 from on High.  / O Lord, glory to You!

kontakion_en.text:
(Original Melody)
Today the Virgin gives birth to the Transcendent One,  /  and the earth offers a cave to the Unapproachable One!  / Angels with shepherds glorify Him.  /  The wise men journey with a star,  /  since for our sake the Pre-Eternal God was born as a young Child.3

matched_oca_saint: Afterfeast of the Nativity of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ

oca_saints.name: Afterfeast of the Nativity of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ

troparion.text: Your Nativity, O Christ our God,  /  has shone to the world the Light of knowledge;  /  for by it, those who worshipped the stars / were taught by a star to adore You,  / the Sun of Righteousness,1  /  and to know You, the Dayspring2 from on High.  / O Lord, glory to You!

kontakion.text:
(Original Melody)
Today the Virgin gives birth to the Transcendent One,  /  and the earth offers a cave to the Unapproachable One!  / Angels with shepherds glorify Him.  /  The wise men journey with a star,  /  since for our sake the Pre-Eternal God was born as a young Child.3

oca_saints.name: 14,000 Infants (the Holy Innocents) slain by Herod at Bethlehem

troparion.text: As acceptable victims and freshly plucked flowers, / as divine first-fruits and newborn lambs, / you were offered to Christ who was born as a child, holy innocents. / You mocked Herod’s wickedness; / now we beseech you: / “Unceasingly pray for our souls.”

kontakion.text: When the King was born in Bethlehem, the Magi came from the east. / Having been guided by the star on high, they brought Him gifts. / But in his exceeding wrath, Herod mowed down the infants as wheat; / lamenting that the rule of his kingdom had come to an end.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Marcellus, Abbot of the Monastery of the “Unsleeping Ones”

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Marcellus, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Mark the Grave-Digger, Theophilus, and John, of the Kiev Near Caves

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy fathers Mark, Theophilus, and John, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Theophilos and Jacob, wonderworkers of Omuch

troparion.text: O God of our Fathers, /  always dealing with us according to Your kindness,  / do not withdraw Your mercy from us,  / but through their prayers,  / direct our lives in peace.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “Seeking the highest...”)
Passing through a multitude of waves unharmed, /  you drowned your bodiless enemies in the streams of your tears, O godly-wise and Venerable Fathers Theophilos and Jacob.  / Since you have received the gift of miracles;  / pray unceasingly for all of us.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Thaddeus the Confessor of the Studion

troparion.text: Champion of Orthodoxy, teacher of purity and of true worship, / the enlightener of the universe and the adornment of hierarchs: / all-wise father Thaddeus, your teachings have gleamed with light upon all things. / Intercede before Christ God to save our souls.

oca_saints.name:
All Orthodox Christians who died as martyrs for the glory of Christ by hunger, thirst, freezing, and by
the sword

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Basilisk the Hesychast of Siberia

troparion.text: Flourishing in the Siberian lands, which you illumined by your exploit of silence,  / you loved the life of the desert until the end, O blessed one;  / and like precious pearls, you received from the Lord the gift of heartfelt prayer,  / and you left us this treasure as an inheritance;  /  accepting it unto salvation we cry to you, O Venerable Basilisk, our Father:  /  “Entreat Christ God to save our souls.”
